details.calculation: ($13,595,371 / 112 total beneficiaries) = $121,387.24 per outcome. | A $1,000 donation achieves ≈ 0.00824 outcomes.

details.elaboration: Quote: 'Anti-Rabies Vaccination (Dogs & Cats) Total = 4310 (In 12 months) April 2023 To March 2024'
details.formula: Highest level of evidence cited in impact claims

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

beneficiaries.location: Kalimpong and Darjeeling, West Bengal, India
Kalimpong and Darjeeling, West Bengal, India

metrics.context_qualifier: Total dogs and cats vaccinated across Kalimpong and Darjeeling in 12 months (April 2023-March 2024).

counterfactual_baseline.description: Rabies is a fatal disease; without vaccination, animals and humans would be at high risk of infection and death.

metrics.evidence_quote: Anti-Rabies Vaccination (Dogs & Cats) Total = 4310 (In 12 months) April 2023 To March 2024

metrics.context_qualifier: Total animals (dogs, cats, birds, pigs, calves) treated at OPDs in Kalimpong and Darjeeling in 12 months (April 2023-March 2024).

counterfactual_baseline.description: Without treatment, sick and injured animals would suffer and likely die. 90% of sick animals made a strong recovery.

metrics.evidence_quote: This year, Kalimpong Animal Shelter treated 5054 animals, while Darjeeling Animal Shelter, operating as its sister unit, treated over 3368 animals, giving them renewed chances at life.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total dogs (1259) and cats (316) sterilized across Kalimpong and Darjeeling in 12 months (April 2023-March 2024).

counterfactual_baseline.description: Uncontrolled stray animal population growth, leading to overpopulation, suffering, and euthanasia.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total dogs rescued by Kalimpong and Darjeeling Animal Shelters in 12 months (April 2023-March 2024).

counterfactual_baseline.description: These dogs would likely have suffered or died from injuries, disease, or neglect without intervention.

metrics.evidence_quote: In 2023, the sister concerns of DGAS - Kalimpong Animal Shelter & Darjeeling Animal Shelter rescued a total of 612 dogs.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total puppies (160) and kittens (33) found loving forever homes in 12 months (April 2023-March 2024).

counterfactual_baseline.description: These animals would remain in shelters or face uncertain futures without adoption.

metrics.evidence_quote: This year, DGAS successfully found loving forever homes to 160 puppies and 33 kittens, giving them a second chance at life.

metrics.context_qualifier: Zero human deaths from rabies reported in Kalimpong and Darjeeling due to DGAS's rabies control efforts.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Rabies was on the rise in India; without intervention, human deaths would likely occur.

metrics.evidence_quote: Notably, there have been zero human deaths from rabies in Kalimpong and Darjeeling, thanks to the commitment of the DGAS team.

metrics.context_qualifier: Achieved in Darjeeling over three years with technical support from Mission Rabies.

counterfactual_baseline.description: Lower vaccination rates would lead to higher rabies risk and potential outbreaks.

metrics.evidence_quote: Over the past three years, with technical support from Mission Rabies, the Darjeeling Animal Shelter has effectively vaccinated canines in every ward and tea estate in Darjeeling, successfully reaching every corner of the community and achieving a vaccination coverage rate of 75%.

significant_events.source_quote: With this, Kalimpong Animal Shelter enacted the ABC Programme in 1998 and a few years later, the same Programme was extended to Darjeeling Goodwill Animal Shelter, a sister shelter.
significant_events.summary: Initiated in 1998 in Kalimpong, extended to Darjeeling in 2008, replacing inhumane poisoning with humane catch, spay/neuter, and release.

significant_events.source_quote: Launched in 2022 with support from Dogs Trust Worldwide, the DAS Rabies Project is built on three core pillars: 1) Intensive Surveillance, 2) Widespread Rabies Awareness and Education 3) Mass Dog Vaccination.
significant_events.summary: Launched in 2022 with Dogs Trust Worldwide, focusing on surveillance, awareness, education, and mass dog vaccination across Darjeeling.

significant_events.source_quote: For the first time, Darjeeling now has its own SPCA, dedicated to tackling these critical issues and advancing animal welfare throughout the region.
significant_events.summary: First-ever SPCA in Darjeeling, supported by HSI India, to address animal cruelty, implement protection policies, and enhance shelter capacity.

significant_events.source_quote: In the wake of this disaster, the Kalimpong Animal Shelter team, as the first animal responders was on the ground providing rapid aid, comfort, and support to these vulnerable animals.
significant_events.summary: Provided rapid aid, rescue, rehabilitation, food, medical care, and vaccinations for animals affected by the Glacial Lake Outburst Flood in October 2023.

significant_events.source_quote: In 2023, Kalimpong Animal Shelter, in collaboration with Help Animals India, saved the lives of 300 community dogs in remote rural areas of Kalimpong through the Village Camp Programme.
significant_events.summary: In collaboration with Help Animals India, saved 300 community dogs in remote rural areas through rabies vaccinations and sterilizations.

significant_events.source_quote: To address these challenges, Kalimpong Animal Shelter, supported by the Marchig Animal Welfare Trust, launched a spay and neuter campaign this year, aimed at helping felines lead healthy and stress-free lives.
significant_events.summary: Supported by Marchig Animal Welfare Trust, launched to address growing feline population, cruelty, abandonment, and accidents.

significant_events.source_quote: Our Humane Education program engages children, promoting values of kindness and respect for animals.
significant_events.summary: Engages children and students to promote kindness, respect for animals, rabies prevention, and dog bite awareness through tours and seminars.

significant_events.event_name: First-Aid Training Programme for Small Animals
significant_events.source_quote: On August 23, 2023, the Darjeeling Animal Shelter (DAS) and Humane Society International (HSI) jointly organised a first aid training session for small animals at the Animal Resources Department GTA in Darjeeling, drawing over 50 animal enthusiasts and advocates from Darjeeling and neighbouring towns.
significant_events.summary: Organized with HSI, trained over 50 animal enthusiasts and advocates in Darjeeling on on-the-spot treatment and first aid.

significant_events.source_quote: The dedicated KAS team, including Vet Dr. Deo and team members Lil, Lakpa, Sanjeev, and Roshan, worked tirelessly until late in the evening, successfully sterilising a total of 37 dogs and 11 cats.
significant_events.summary: Organized with Superintendent of Police, successfully sterilized 37 dogs and 11 cats (48 animals received care) for free.

data.original_text: Founded by Dr. Christine Townend in the year 1992 for the sole purpose of Animal Birth Control of street dogs that were suffering with genital problems , skin conditions and overburdened with a litter of puppies that were neither adopted nor fostered.
The female dogs and litter of puppies had to succumb a life of despair and cruelty because the locals feared of rabies and female dogs procreating time and again.
About 22 years ago, rabies, a fatal disease/virus was on the rise in India and in these two Indian districts under West Bengal, Kalimpong and Darjeeling.

The Municipality unanimously agreed to this approach then a new hope of positivity dawned on Dr. Christine by starting an animal shelter with a dispensary set-up for the near future well-being of street dogs where animals could be treated, rescued, caught for sterilisation and sheltered post-operative care for a few days and then be released.
With this, Kalimpong Animal Shelter enacted the ABC Programme in 1998 and a few years later, the same Programme was extended to Darjeeling Goodwill Animal Shelter, a sister shelter.
Dog creche providing healthcare, sterilization, and vaccination services to improve the lives of street dogs.
The shelter started an Animal Birth Control (ABC) program, focusing on spaying and vaccinating dogs.
The staff faced challenges in identifying stray dogs from owned ones, leading to an education campaign to locate dog owners and promote the benefits of spaying.
Over the years so many dogs have been spayed and vaccinated that, not only has the health of the community dog population greatly increased, but the population of puppies has been greatly reduced.
These days there are so few puppies available in Kalimpong town, that Darjeeling Animal Shelter sends surplus pups from Darjeeling to KAS for adoption.
In addition to the ABC programme, the staff at KAS conducted many camps in far-lying villages only accessible by walking.
KAS has saved the lives of thousands of animals since its foundation in 1993.
Due to the work of DAS and KAS, together with the Darjeeling and Kalimpong Municipalities, all poisoning of street dogs has stopped from 1998 onwards.
The street dogs are now safe and friendly.
Puppies are not being born to die an early death from starvation and disease.

The Chairman of both Darjeeling and Kalimpong Municipalities agreed to stop poisoning all dogs and since then KAS and DAS have successfully carried out ABC programmes. No further dogs in either Municipality have been poisoned.
